description:
## Goal

Remove the 16-byte HeapHeader from list cons cells and use a distinct pointer tag (TAG_LIST = 0b100) to identify them, matching the BEAM's approach. This halves the per-cell allocation from 32 bytes to 16 bytes.

## Background

fz cons cells are currently 32 bytes: a 16-byte HeapHeader followed by head (8 bytes) and tail (8 bytes). The BEAM achieves 16-byte cons cells by encoding the cell type in the pointer tag rather than in a per-cell header, eliminating the header entirely.

fz has three reserved tag slots (0b100–0b110) available. Using 0b100 for list pointers gives us the same trick.

## GC Forwarding: In-Band TAG_FWD

Headerless cons cells cannot use the existing in-place forwarding scheme (FORWARDED_KIND at offset 0, to-space ptr at offset 8), because offsets 0 and 8 are live data (head and tail).

The solution is an in-band forwarding tag: TAG_FWD = 0b110. It is internal to the collector and never visible to user code.

When Cheney copies a cons cell:
1. Copy the 16 bytes verbatim to to-space.
2. Overwrite the head word (offset 0) of the from-space cell with `(to_space_ptr as u64) | TAG_FWD`.

When the scanner encounters a TAG_LIST pointer, it reads word 0 of the from-space cell before copying. If that word has TAG_FWD, the cell was already copied — extract the to-space address from the payload and rewrite the slot. No copy needed.

This matches the BEAM's approach. No per-GC HashMap, no extra allocation, no two-class scan logic.

## Changes Required

### runtime/src/fz_value.rs
- Add TAG_LIST = 0b100 and TAG_FWD = 0b110 (internal; never exposed as Tag enum variant)
- Shrink ListCons: drop header field; head at offset 0, tail at offset 8
- Update unbox_ptr() to recognise both TAG_PTR (0b000) and TAG_LIST (0b100)
- Update render_list(): replace h.kind check with tag-bit check (v.0 & TAG_MASK == TAG_LIST)

### runtime/src/heap.rs
- alloc_list_cons: allocate 16 bytes instead of 32, write no header, tag returned pointer with TAG_LIST
- cheney_forward: detect TAG_LIST pointers; check head word for TAG_FWD before copying; write TAG_FWD forwarding marker into head on copy
- cheney_trace_children (List branch): change offsets 16/24 → 0/8
- deep_copy_value (List branch): change offsets 16/24 → 0/8
- forward_field: handle TAG_LIST in addition to TAG_PTR

### src/ir_codegen.rs
- ListHead load: change offset 16 → 0
- ListTail load: change offset 24 → 8

## Non-Changes
- src/ir_lower.rs: no changes — operates on Prim ops with no offset knowledge
- src/fz_ir.rs: no changes
- AOT path: shares codegen, picks up the offset fix automatically

## Verification
- All existing list tests must pass
- Add a test that allocates a large list and measures heap bytes consumed, asserting 16 bytes per cell
- Three-path parity: interpreter, JIT, AOT all exercise the new layout
title: fz: shrink cons cells from 32 to 16 bytes
